commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.6.2: the bind — the scope in X-Y mode, scroll & animation locked on the diagonal

Reskin the Observe movement's bind node from the .bd-* wiring diagram into the
CRT oscilloscope in X-Y (Lissajous) mode, making the movement cohesive with its
trigger sibling (PR #22). CH1 scroll drives the X axis, CH2 animation drives the
Y axis, and a phosphor dot rides the y=x diagonal — because both coordinates ARE
the one real p, a straight diagonal IS the proof scroll and animation are bound
1:1 (the animation-timeline:scroll() idea).

- Reuses trigger's CRT shell (.sc + .sc-trace + chrome); only the trace geometry,
  the faint ideal guide line, and the projection crosshairs are added (.xy-*).
- [LAW:one-source-of-truth] x and y are the SAME p; the lock is read off
  Delta=|x-y| (structurally 0), never a second fabricated signal. The diagonal is
  a consequence of the data, not an asserted decoration.
- [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] paint writes state only; the dot's glow is a CSS
  @keyframes the global reduced-motion kill stops.
- Removes the now-dead .bd-* CSS block.

Verified in Chrome DevTools across the node's full scroll range: dot tracks the
diagonal, clip reveals up to it, X=Y=p / Delta=0.00 / LOCKED 1:1, no page errors.

mit-styling-aj1.6.1: the trigger — IntersectionObserver as an oscilloscope (#22)

Reskin the trigger node into a CRT oscilloscope: the step events are a
phosphor pulse train on a measured graticule, the beam sweeps with scroll,
and each pulse crossing the TRIGGER LEVEL line (the rootMargin) latches its
onStep. The scope's trigger IS the IntersectionObserver's threshold.

- [LAW:one-source-of-truth] the trigger level is authored once (--sc-trig):
  it sets the dashed line's top AND the SVG pulse geometry (peak<trig<base),
  so 'beam swept past pulse i' IS 'pulse i crossed the trigger' — the fired
  latch derives from that one geometric fact, no parallel threshold to drift.
- [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] the trace is built once from step data;
  paint only reveals it via clip-path and reads which centres the beam passed.
- Decorative phosphor flicker is a CSS @keyframes, auto-gated by the global
  prefers-reduced-motion kill. paint(p,n) writes state only.
- Establishes the Observe movement's CRT signature, shared with bind; no
  other section wears it.

Verified in Chrome across the full scroll range (armed 0/5 -> 2/5 -> 5/5),
zero console errors.
